name: General Geography
description: Countries, landmarks, oceans, and world geography

questions.q: Which country is the largest in the world by land area?

source.notes: About 17.1 million km² — spans 11 time zones.

questions.q: Which is the highest mountain in the world?

source.notes: About 8,849 metres (29,029 ft).

questions.q: What nickname do New Zealanders affectionately go by, named after a fuzzy native bird?

source.notes: The kiwi is New Zealand's national bird.

questions.q: How do Spaniards spell the name of their own country in Spanish?

source.notes: Note the tilde over the 'n'.

questions.q: The 'Black Forest', or Schwarzwald, is a mountainous forest in which country?

source.notes: In the southwestern German state of Baden-Württemberg.

questions.q: What is the currency used in the United Kingdom?

source.notes: Pound sterling; symbol £.

questions.q: The famous Table Mountain overlooks which city?

source.notes: Declared one of the New 7 Wonders of Nature in 2011.

questions.q: What is the second-highest mountain peak on Earth?

source.notes: 8,611 m; located in the Karakoram on the China–Pakistan border.

questions.q: How many time zones does Russia cover?

source.notes: Spans more longitude than any other country.

questions.q: What is the largest island in the world (excluding continental Australia)?

source.notes: About 2.17 million km².

questions.q: What is Esperanto, invented in 1887?

source.notes: Created by L.L. Zamenhof as an easy-to-learn neutral second language.

questions.q: The famous department store Harrods is located in which city?

source.notes: Knightsbridge, London; founded 1849.

questions.q: In which sea can you find the Dodecanese islands, including Rhodes and Kos?

source.notes: 'Dodecanese' means 'Twelve Islands' in Greek.

questions.q: The Rijksmuseum and Anne Frank House are major attractions in which European capital?

source.notes: Dutch national museum and Frank family's WWII hiding place.

questions.q: The Yoruba people are a major ethnic group from which region of Africa?

source.notes: Primarily in southwestern Nigeria and Benin.

questions.q: Which country surpassed China as the world's most populous country in 2023?

source.notes: India's population reached ~1.43 billion in 2023.

questions.q: Fez and Tangier are major cities in which North African country?

source.notes: Along with Casablanca, Rabat, and Marrakesh.

questions.q: The capital of Brazil is what city, which replaced Rio de Janeiro in 1960?

source.notes: Purpose-built capital designed by Oscar Niemeyer.

questions.q: The Volga, Europe's longest river, flows almost entirely within which country?

source.notes: About 3,530 km long; empties into the Caspian Sea.

questions.q: Mount Fuji, an active stratovolcano and Japan's tallest peak, stands at roughly what height?

source.notes: Last erupted in 1707; about 100 km southwest of Tokyo.

questions.q: The Strait of Magellan, linking the Atlantic and Pacific, is at the southern tip of which continent?

source.notes: Between the mainland and Tierra del Fuego; named for Ferdinand Magellan.

questions.q: The Commonwealth of Nations is an association of mostly former colonies of which country?

source.notes: 56 member states; successor to the British Empire.
